     1	# LMDB sub-design: `EventStore` trait
     2	
     3	> Part of [`docs/design/lmdb-schema.md`](../lmdb-schema.md).
     4	> Supporting types are in [`trait/types.md`](trait/types.md) (extracted to keep this file ≤ 300 LOC).
     5	
     6	## 1. Crate placement
     7	
     8	`crates/nmp-core/src/store/events.rs` (filename note: `trait` is a Rust keyword, so the file is named `events.rs` and exposes `pub trait EventStore`). Re-exported from `nmp_core::store::EventStore`. The actor (`crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`) holds the store as `store: Box<dyn EventStore>`; backends are constructed by the factory in `store/mod.rs::open_event_store(&AppConfig) -> Result<Box<dyn EventStore>, StoreError>`.
     9	
    10	All types referenced below (`InsertOutcome`, `TombstoneRow`, `WatermarkKey`, `ClaimerId`, `StoreError`, etc.) are defined in [`trait/types.md`](trait/types.md) and live in `crates/nmp-core/src/store/types.rs`.
    11	
    12	## 2. The trait
    13	
    14	```rust
    15	pub trait EventStore: Send + Sync {
    16	    // ─────── Reads ───────
    17	
    18	    /// Primary lookup. Returns Ok(None) if absent; tombstones do not count as "present".
    19	    fn get_by_id(&self, id: &EventId) -> Result<Option<StoredEvent>, StoreError>;
    20	
    21	    /// `idx_author_kind` scan, newest-first.
    22	    /// `kinds` must be non-empty; callers wanting any-kind use `scan_by_kind_time` instead.
    23	    fn scan_by_author_kind<'a>(
    24	        &'a self,
    25	        author: &PubKey,
    26	        kinds: &[u32],
    27	        since: Option<u64>,
    28	        until: Option<u64>,
    29	        limit: usize,
    30	    ) -> Result<Box<dyn EventIter + 'a>, StoreError>;
    31	
    32	    /// `idx_kind_dtag` lookup. Returns the current authoritative parameterized
    33	    /// replaceable for `(pubkey, kind, d_tag)`, or Ok(None).
    34	    fn get_param_replaceable(
    35	        &self,
    36	        pubkey: &PubKey,
    37	        kind: u32,
    38	        d_tag: &[u8],
    39	    ) -> Result<Option<StoredEvent>, StoreError>;
    40	
    41	    /// `idx_kind_dtag_time` scan, newest-first across all authors for `(kind, d_tag)`.
    42	    /// Used for global parameterized-replaceable discovery (e.g. "recent articles with slug X").
    43	    /// To list all replaceables by a specific author use `scan_by_author_kind` instead.
    44	    fn scan_by_kind_dtag<'a>(
    45	        &'a self,
    46	        kind: u32,
    47	        d_tag: &[u8],
    48	        since: Option<u64>,
    49	        until: Option<u64>,
    50	        limit: usize,
    51	    ) -> Result<Box<dyn EventIter + 'a>, StoreError>;
    52	
    53	    /// `idx_etag_time` scan, newest-first. Used by reaction / repost / thread views.
    54	    /// `kinds` must be non-empty; pass `&[7]` for reactions, `&[6]` for reposts, etc.
    55	    fn scan_by_etag<'a>(
    56	        &'a self,
    57	        target: &EventId,
    58	        kinds: &[u32],
    59	        limit: usize,
    60	    ) -> Result<Box<dyn EventIter + 'a>, StoreError>;
    61	
    62	    /// `idx_ptag_time` scan, newest-first. Used by notifications / mention views.
    63	    /// `kinds` must be non-empty.
    64	    fn scan_by_ptag<'a>(
    65	        &'a self,
    66	        target: &PubKey,
    67	        kinds: &[u32],
    68	        limit: usize,
    69	    ) -> Result<Box<dyn EventIter + 'a>, StoreError>;
    70	
    71	    /// `idx_kind_time` scan, newest-first. Pass `kinds = &[]` to scan all kinds
    72	    /// (the only scan method that accepts an empty kinds slice).
    73	    fn scan_by_kind_time<'a>(
    74	        &'a self,
    75	        kinds: &[u32],
    76	        since: Option<u64>,
    77	        until: Option<u64>,
    78	        limit: usize,
    79	    ) -> Result<Box<dyn EventIter + 'a>, StoreError>;
    80	
    81	    /// `idx_expires` scan, ascending — used by the NIP-40 reaper.
    82	    fn scan_expiring_before<'a>(
    83	        &'a self,
    84	        unix_seconds: u64,
    85	        limit: usize,
    86	    ) -> Result<Box<dyn EventIter + 'a>, StoreError>;
    87	
    88	    /// Tombstones referencing a target id (typically one row).
    89	    fn tombstones_for(&self, target: &EventId) -> Result<Vec<TombstoneRow>, StoreError>;
    90	
    91	    /// Iterate all tombstones (used by `nmp dump`).
    92	    fn list_tombstones<'a>(&'a self)
    93	        -> Result<Box<dyn Iterator<Item = Result<TombstoneRow, StoreError>> + Send + 'a>, StoreError>;
    94	
    95	    /// Provenance sidecar for an event.
    96	    fn provenance_for(&self, id: &EventId) -> Result<Vec<ProvenanceEntry>, StoreError>;
    97	
    98	    // ─────── Writes ───────
    99	
   100	    /// The single insert path. `source` is the relay that delivered this copy.
   101	    /// Verifies signature/delegation, applies §7.1 invariants, updates secondaries
   102	    /// + provenance + tombstones atomically. Returns InsertOutcome per §7.1.
   103	    fn insert(&self, event: nostr::Event, source: &RelayUrl, received_at_ms: u64)
   104	        -> Result<InsertOutcome, StoreError>;
   105	
   106	    /// Delete by a NMP-internal filter — for admin / GC / kind:5 application.
   107	    /// Returns the number of primary rows removed.
   108	    fn delete_by_filter(&self, filter: DeleteFilter) -> Result<usize, StoreError>;
   109	
   110	    // ─────── Watermarks ───────
   111	
   112	    fn read_watermark(&self, key: &WatermarkKey) -> Result<Option<WatermarkRow>, StoreError>;
   113	    fn write_watermark(&self, row: WatermarkRow) -> Result<(), StoreError>;
   114	
   115	    /// Returns the coverage classification for a `(filter, relay)` pair
   116	    /// based on the stored watermark row and the configured staleness window.
   117	    /// Used by the M2 subscription planner to decide whether a cache miss is
   118	    /// authoritative (no need to fetch) or requires a new REQ.
   119	    fn coverage(&self, key: &WatermarkKey) -> Result<Coverage, StoreError>;
   120	
   121	    /// Iterate watermarks for a specific relay. O(matching rows) — backed by the
   122	    /// `idx_watermark_relay` secondary index (see [`keys.md`](keys.md) §5).
   123	    fn list_watermarks_for_relay<'a>(
   124	        &'a self,
   125	        relay_url: &str,
   126	    ) -> Result<Box<dyn Iterator<Item = Result<WatermarkRow, StoreError>> + Send + 'a>, StoreError>;
   127	
   128	    // ─────── Hot-set / claims (GC) ───────
   129	
   130	    /// Register the maximum number of events this view is allowed to pin at once.
   131	    /// Must be called before `claim()` for a given `claimer`. If not called,
   132	    /// the store applies a default per-view ceiling of `max_claim_per_view` events
   133	    /// (see [`gc.md`](gc.md) §2 — default 1 000).
   134	    ///
   135	    /// Enforcement: `claim()` counts the current per-claimer set size; if adding
   136	    /// `ids` would exceed this budget OR the global `max_pinned_total` ceiling,
   137	    /// it returns `Err(StoreError::OverPinned { ... })` without modifying state.
   138	    /// The caller is responsible for releasing stale claims first.
   139	    ///
   140	    /// Rationale: D8 (reactivity contract) requires that the kernel's working-set

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# LMDB sub-design: key encoding
     2	
     3	> Part of [`docs/design/lmdb-schema.md`](../lmdb-schema.md). Fixes the byte layout of every sub-database the NMP store opens. Primary event storage is owned by `nostr-lmdb`; everything below is NMP-owned and lives in NMP-managed sub-databases under the same `lmdb::Environment` (per open question 1 in the master doc).
     4	
     5	## 1. LMDB environment layout
     6	
     7	One `lmdb::Environment` per app data directory. Sub-databases:
     8	
     9	| Sub-db | Owner | Key shape | Value | Notes |
    10	|---|---|---|---|---|
    11	| (multiple) | `nostr-lmdb` | upstream | upstream | event primary, internal filter indexes, kind:5 suppression |
    12	| `idx_author_kind` | NMP | `pubkey[32] ‖ kind_be[4] ‖ created_at_desc_be[8] ‖ event_id[32]` | empty | newest-first scans for `(author, kinds[])` |
    13	| `idx_kind_dtag` | NMP | `kind_be[4] ‖ pubkey[32] ‖ dtag_len_be[2] ‖ dtag_bytes` | `event_id[32]` | parameterized replaceable exact-key lookup (by author) |
    14	| `idx_kind_dtag_time` | NMP | `kind_be[4] ‖ dtag_len_be[2] ‖ dtag_bytes ‖ created_at_desc_be[8] ‖ event_id[32]` | empty | newest-first scan by `(kind, d_tag)` across all authors |
    15	| `idx_etag_time` | NMP | `target_event_id[32] ‖ created_at_desc_be[8] ‖ event_id[32]` | `kind_be[4]` | reaction/reply/thread view scans |
    16	| `idx_ptag_time` | NMP | `target_pubkey[32] ‖ created_at_desc_be[8] ‖ event_id[32]` | `kind_be[4]` | mentions / notifications |
    17	| `idx_kind_time` | NMP | `kind_be[4] ‖ created_at_desc_be[8] ‖ event_id[32]` | empty | global-by-kind backfills |
    18	| `idx_expires` | NMP | `expires_at_be[8] ‖ event_id[32]` | empty | NIP-40 reaper |
    19	| `tombstones` | NMP | `target_event_id[32]` | CBOR `TombstoneRow` | persists past delete (event-id keyed) |
    20	| `tombstones_addr` | NMP | `pubkey[32] ‖ kind_be[4] ‖ dtag_len_be[2] ‖ dtag_bytes` | CBOR `TombstoneRow` | address tombstones for kind:5 `a` tags that arrive before the target event |
    21	| `provenance` | NMP | `event_id[32]` | CBOR `ProvenanceRow` | per-relay sidecar (master doc §9) |
    22	| `watermarks` | NMP | `filter_hash[32] ‖ relay_url_bytes` | CBOR `WatermarkRow` | M4 NIP-77 sync state |
    23	| `idx_watermark_relay` | NMP | `relay_url_bytes ‖ filter_hash[32]` | empty | relay-first secondary; enables O(matching rows) `list_watermarks_for_relay` |
    24	| `claims_meta` | NMP | `claimer_id_be[8]` | CBOR `BTreeSet<EventId>` | pinned set per ClaimerId (deduped); rebuilt on restart from open views |
    25	| `domain_<ns>_data` | NMP, per `DomainModule` | module-defined | module-defined | one sub-db per registered namespace |
    26	| `domain_<ns>_idx_<name>` | NMP, per `DomainModule` index | `index_key ‖ primary_key` | empty | secondary indexes per `DomainIndex` |
    27	| `_meta` | NMP | string namespace | `{ schema_version: u32, opened_with_nmp_version: String }` | migration tracking |
    28	
    29	Sub-databases are opened lazily on first access and cached on the `LmdbEventStore`.
    30	
    31	## 2. Endian + ordering conventions
    32	
    33	- All integers in keys are **big-endian** so LMDB's byte-wise comparator matches numeric order.
    34	- `created_at_desc_be = (u64::MAX - created_at).to_be_bytes()` so a forward scan returns newest-first without `MDB_PREV` gymnastics.
    35	- All pubkeys / event ids are fixed-width 32 bytes; the `nostr` crate's `EventId` and `PublicKey` give us byte arrays directly.
    36	
    37	## 3. Secondary index details
    38	
    39	### 3.1 `idx_author_kind`
    40	
    41	Key: `pubkey[32] ‖ kind_be[4] ‖ created_at_desc_be[8] ‖ event_id[32]` → empty value.
    42	
    43	Scan recipes:
    44	
    45	- *Newest N events by author* — `range(pubkey ‖ 0u32_be ‖ ..)` (kind=0 lower bound) up to `pubkey ‖ u32::MAX_be ‖ ..`, take N.
    46	- *Newest N events by `(author, kind=1)`* — `range(pubkey ‖ 1u32_be ‖ ..)` up to `pubkey ‖ 1u32_be ‖ u64::MAX_be`, take N.
    47	- *All kind:0 for author* — `range(pubkey ‖ 0u32_be ‖ ..)`, take 1 (because the replaceable index ensures only one).
    48	
    49	Replaceable supersession (§7.1): on insert of a new kind in [0, 3, 10000–19999], find existing row via this index with `(pubkey, kind)` prefix, compare `created_at`, if incoming wins delete old + write new. Both deletes happen in the same `RwTxn` as the new write so there is no half-state visible to readers.
    50	
    51	### 3.2 `idx_kind_dtag` (parameterized replaceable)
    52	
    53	Key: `kind_be[4] ‖ pubkey[32] ‖ dtag_len_be[2] ‖ dtag_bytes` → `event_id[32]`.
    54	
    55	The d-tag bytes go last so two events with the same `(kind, pubkey)` but different `d` tags don't collide; the explicit length prefix avoids `d="foo"` vs `d="foob"` aliasing under prefix scans. Lookup is exact-key: `get_param_replaceable(pubkey, kind, d_tag)` builds the key and reads.
    56	
    57	The value is the `event_id`; the primary event itself lives in the `nostr-lmdb` events sub-db. On supersession, the old event-id is fetched from this row, both primary and old `idx_*` rows are deleted, and the value is overwritten with the new id.
    58	
    59	### 3.3 `idx_kind_dtag_time` (parameterized replaceable — time-ordered)
    60	
    61	Key: `kind_be[4] ‖ dtag_len_be[2] ‖ dtag_bytes ‖ created_at_desc_be[8] ‖ event_id[32]` → empty.
    62	
    63	Enables newest-first scans across **all authors** for a `(kind, d_tag)` pair — the use case is "find the most recent article with slug `my-post` across all authors" (kind:30023 global search). This is distinct from `idx_kind_dtag` which is exact-key by author.
    64	
    65	Write cost: 2 extra LMDB writes per parameterized-replaceable insert (one for `idx_kind_dtag` + one for `idx_kind_dtag_time`). Only events in 30000–39999 range generate this entry; overhead is proportional to parameterized-replaceable volume.
    66	
    67	Scan recipe: `range(kind_be ‖ dtag_len_be ‖ dtag_bytes ‖ ..)` forward — yields newest-first thanks to `created_at_desc_be`.
    68	
    69	### 3.4 `idx_etag_time` and `idx_ptag_time`
    70	
    71	Key: `target[32] ‖ created_at_desc_be[8] ‖ event_id[32]` → `kind_be[4]`.
    72	
    73	The value holds the kind so a reactions view can filter `(kinds == 7)` during scan without a primary-row fetch per candidate. Bookmark / repost / thread views similarly avoid the `get_by_id` round trip until they need the body.
    74	
    75	On insert, the kernel walks the event's `tags`: every `e` tag value goes into `idx_etag_time` and every `p` tag value goes into `idx_ptag_time`. Tag values must be 32-byte hex (validated at insert time); non-conformant tags are silently skipped from indexing (they are still stored in the event body).
    76	
    77	### 3.5 `idx_kind_time`
    78	
    79	Key: `kind_be[4] ‖ created_at_desc_be[8] ‖ event_id[32]` → empty.
    80	
    81	Used by *global-by-kind* backfills (e.g. "recent kind:0 across all authors" during diagnostics). Heavy index — populated for **all** kinds by default but the implementation may skip kinds in a configurable deny-list to keep write amplification down (default deny-list: kind:1 if config flag `index_kind1_globally=false`, which it is by default; M2's planner does not need a global kind:1 scan).
    82	
    83	### 3.6 `idx_expires`
    84	
    85	Key: `expires_at_be[8] ‖ event_id[32]` → empty.
    86	
    87	Populated **only** for events that have an `expiration` tag at insert (NIP-40). `gc_step()` opens a read cursor at `expires_at = 0`, walks forward up to the configured budget, and reaps any keys whose `expires_at ≤ now_unix_seconds()`. Each reaped event triggers a tombstone-of-origin `NIP40Expiry` write so re-insertions (from a re-sync) don't resurrect it.
    88	
    89	## 4. Tombstones
    90

     1	# LMDB sub-design: watermarks, provenance, migrations
     2	
     3	> Part of [`docs/design/lmdb-schema.md`](../lmdb-schema.md). Covers the three NMP-owned LMDB sub-databases that hold non-event durable state: `watermarks`, `provenance`, and the per-`DomainModule` sub-databases tracked by `_meta`.
     4	
     5	## 1. Watermark table
     6	
     7	Sub-db: `watermarks`. Populated by the M4 NIP-77 negentropy engine and consulted by the M2 subscription planner (per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2 "coverage-aware backfill").
     8	
     9	Key: `filter_hash[32] ‖ relay_url_bytes` — exact-key lookups only; no scans by `filter_hash` alone are needed (the planner always knows the relay it's about to query). The relay url is appended raw (no length prefix) because lookups are exact-key and the trailing bytes have no semantic role beyond identification.
    10	
    11	Value: CBOR `WatermarkRow`:
    12	
    13	```rust
    14	#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug)]
    15	pub struct WatermarkRow {
    16	    pub key: WatermarkKey,
    17	    pub synced_up_to: u64,                 // unix seconds
    18	    pub last_sync_method: SyncMethod,      // Negentropy | ReqScan | Manual
    19	    pub last_negentropy_state: Option<Vec<u8>>, // engine-opaque resume blob (M4)
    20	    pub bytes_saved_vs_req: u64,           // cumulative; for diagnostics
    21	    pub updated_at: u64,                   // unix seconds
    22	}
    23	```
    24	
    25	`last_negentropy_state` is an opaque byte blob written by the NIP-77 engine (M4) — the store does not interpret it. Empty for `ReqScan` / `Manual` rows.
    26	
    27	### 1.1 Authoritative cache-miss semantics
    28	
    29	Per §7.1 of the spec: "A cache-miss query against a fully-synced `(filter, relay)` pair is **authoritative**." The store implements this via the read path:
    30	
    31	```rust
    32	pub fn coverage(&self, key: &WatermarkKey) -> Result<Coverage, StoreError> {
    33	    let now_s = unix_now();
    34	    match self.read_watermark(key)? {
    35	        Some(row) if row.synced_up_to >= now_s.saturating_sub(self.cfg.coverage_staleness_secs) =>
    36	            Ok(Coverage::CompleteAsOf(row.synced_up_to)),
    37	        Some(row) => Ok(Coverage::PartialUpTo(row.synced_up_to)),
    38	        None => Ok(Coverage::Unknown),
    39	    }
    40	    // On I/O error the `?` propagates; callers treat Err as Unknown (do not lie about completeness).
    41	}
    42	```
    43	
    44	`now_s` is derived internally from the system clock — callers do not need to supply it. `coverage_staleness_secs` defaults to 300 s — a row that hasn't been re-confirmed in 5 minutes is treated as partial. The planner uses this signal to decide whether a missing-event query is "doesn't exist" (CompleteAsOf) or "need to fetch" (PartialUpTo / Unknown).
    45	
    46	### 1.2 Restart hydration
    47	
    48	On `LmdbEventStore::open()`, the store reads all `watermarks` rows and builds an in-memory `HashMap<WatermarkKey, WatermarkRow>` for hot lookups. Every `write_watermark` updates both the in-memory map and the LMDB row in a single `RwTxn`. Restart re-derives the map; we don't need a separate cache file.
    49	
    50	For installations with O(100k+) watermarks (an edge case — typical apps see O(100)–O(10k)), the in-memory map switches to a lazy-loaded variant that pages on demand. Threshold and switching logic deferred to M4 when the negentropy engine's real-world row count is measured.
    51	
    52	## 2. Provenance
    53	
    54	Sub-db: `provenance`. Per-event sidecar; the master doc §9 justifies the split-table choice.
    55	

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# LMDB sub-design: GC working-set policy
     2	
     3	> Part of [`docs/design/lmdb-schema.md`](../lmdb-schema.md). Formalises the hot-resident / cold-on-disk split required by ADR-0003 (`docs/decisions/0003-working-set-memory.md`).
     4	
     5	## 1. Definitions
     6	
     7	```
     8	stored_events = every event currently in `events` (primary), not tombstoned
     9	
    10	claim_pinned  = ⋃ { ids | ids ∈ claims[claimer] for each registered claimer }
    11	                where each `claimer` is an open ViewHandle / open ActionHandle
    12	
    13	open_view_cover = ⋃ { dependency_target_ids(spec)
    14	                       | (view_id, spec) ∈ open_views }
    15	                  computed from the composite reverse-index per ADR-0001
    16	
    17	recently_touched = top-N by `last_touched_ms` (default N = 10,000)
    18	
    19	hot_resident = claim_pinned ∪ open_view_cover ∪ recently_touched
    20	cold         = stored_events \ hot_resident
    21	```
    22	
    23	`last_touched_ms` is bumped on every `get_by_id`, on every secondary scan that *materialises* the event body, and on `insert` for a fresh row. Scans that only return ids/timestamps (e.g., the early-filter pass in a view's planner) do **not** bump it — only the construction of a `Delta` payload that needs the body does.
    24	
    25	`hot_resident` is stored in memory; `cold` lives only on disk. The store still **knows** about every cold event via secondaries — the reverse index covers both per ADR-0003: "The reverse index indexes both hot and cold events. Lookup returns view ids immediately; event bodies for delta construction load lazily and synchronously via the storage backend."
    26	
    27	## 2. Hot data structure
    28	
    29	```rust
    30	pub(crate) struct HotSet {
    31	    // LRU bounded by `target_hot_size` (default 10,000), evicts non-pinned.
    32	    lru: lru::LruCache<EventId, Arc<nostr::Event>>,
    33	    // Strong-pin overlay; refcounted by ClaimerId.
    34	    pinned: HashMap<EventId, u32>,                   // event_id → refcount
    35	    // Reverse map for cheap release(); BTreeSet ensures claim() is idempotent per claimer.
    36	    by_claimer: HashMap<ClaimerId, BTreeSet<EventId>>,
    37	    // Per-view ceiling registered by register_view_cover().
    38	    view_budgets: HashMap<ClaimerId, usize>,
    39	    target_hot_size: usize,
    40	    // Ceilings (enforced on every claim() call — D8 / ADR-0001..0004).
    41	    max_claim_per_view: usize,   // default 1_000; callers may lower via register_view_cover
    42	    max_pinned_total: usize,     // default 20_000; hard cap on pinned.len()
    43	}
    44	
    45	impl HotSet {
    46	    /// Record the budget for a view before its first claim. If not called, the
    47	    /// default `max_claim_per_view` applies. Calling it again with a lower budget
    48	    /// after claims have already been issued does *not* retroactively reject them;
    49	    /// the lower ceiling applies to future claim() calls.
    50	    pub fn register_view_cover(&mut self, c: ClaimerId, budget: usize) {
    51	        self.view_budgets.insert(c, budget);
    52	    }
    53	
    54	    /// Pin `ids` for `c`. Idempotent: re-claiming an id already in the claimer's set
    55	    /// is a no-op (refcount not double-incremented). Budget checks count only genuinely
    56	    /// new ids. Returns `StoreError::OverPinned` if limits would be exceeded.
    57	    /// On rejection, the state is unchanged (all-or-nothing per call).
    58	    pub fn claim(&mut self, c: ClaimerId, ids: &[EventId]) -> Result<(), StoreError> {
    59	        let existing = self.by_claimer.get(&c);
    60	        // Count only ids that are new to this claimer (dedup by BTreeSet membership).
    61	        let new_ids: Vec<EventId> = ids.iter()
    62	            .filter(|id| existing.map_or(true, |s| !s.contains(*id)))
    63	            .copied()
    64	            .collect();
    65	        let per_view_ceiling = self.view_budgets
    66	            .get(&c)
    67	            .copied()
    68	            .unwrap_or(self.max_claim_per_view);
    69	        let current_for_claimer = existing.map_or(0, |s| s.len());
    70	        if current_for_claimer + new_ids.len() > per_view_ceiling {
    71	            return Err(StoreError::OverPinned {
    72	                claimer: c,
    73	                requested: current_for_claimer + new_ids.len(),
    74	                ceiling: per_view_ceiling,
    75	            });
    76	        }
    77	        let new_global = self.pinned.len() + new_ids.iter()
    78	            .filter(|id| !self.pinned.contains_key(*id))
    79	            .count();
    80	        if new_global > self.max_pinned_total {
    81	            return Err(StoreError::OverPinned {
    82	                claimer: c,
    83	                requested: new_global,
    84	                ceiling: self.max_pinned_total,
    85	            });
    86	        }
    87	        let set = self.by_claimer.entry(c).or_default();
    88	        for id in &new_ids {
    89	            set.insert(*id);
    90	            *self.pinned.entry(*id).or_insert(0) += 1;
    91	        }
    92	        Ok(())
    93	    }
    94	
    95	    pub fn release(&mut self, c: ClaimerId) {
    96	        if let Some(ids) = self.by_claimer.remove(&c) {
    97	            for id in ids {
    98	                if let Some(rc) = self.pinned.get_mut(&id) {
    99	                    *rc = rc.saturating_sub(1);
   100	                    if *rc == 0 { self.pinned.remove(&id); }
   101	                }
   102	            }
   103	        }
   104	        self.view_budgets.remove(&c);
   105	    }
   106	
   107	    pub fn touch(&mut self, id: EventId, e: Arc<nostr::Event>) {
   108	        self.lru.put(id, e);                          // bumps LRU
   109	        self.trim();
   110	    }
   111	
   112	    fn trim(&mut self) {
   113	        while self.lru.len() > self.target_hot_size {
   114	            // pop_lru returns oldest; skip pinned ones until we find an evictable.
   115	            // (LruCache::pop_lru doesn't take a predicate; we rotate.)

    60	
    61	Each file is bounded ≤ 300 LOC per AGENTS.md. The trait module is read by the actor; backend modules are read only by the orchestrator.
    62	
    63	## 3. EventStore trait
    64	
    65	See [`lmdb/trait.md`](lmdb/trait.md) for the exact `pub trait EventStore` signature with all required methods, return types, and the `StoreError` enum. Summary:
    66	
    67	- **Reads:** `get_by_id`, `scan_by_author_kind`, `get_param_replaceable`, `scan_by_kind_dtag`, `scan_by_etag`, `scan_by_ptag`, `scan_by_kind_time`, `scan_expiring_before`. All `scan_*` methods return a streaming `EventIter` so the planner pages without materialising. Cache-coverage queries via `coverage(WatermarkKey)` return a `Coverage` enum (`CompleteAsOf`, `PartialUpTo`, `Unknown`) used by the M2 planner.
    68	- **Writes:** `insert(event, RelayUrl)` returns `InsertOutcome` matching §7.1's table. `delete_by_filter` for foreign-relay cleanups. `tombstones_for` for replay.
    69	- **Watermarks / sync:** `read_watermark`, `write_watermark`, `list_watermarks_for_relay`.
    70	- **GC:** `claim(ClaimerId, &[EventId])`, `release(ClaimerId)`, `hot_set_hint(&[EventId])`, `gc_step(GcBudget) -> GcReport`.
    71	- **Domain rows:** `domain_open(namespace) -> DomainHandle` returns a typed handle; `DomainHandle::put/get/scan_index` is the per-DomainModule API.
    72	- **Migration:** `run_migrations(&[DomainMigration])` runs at startup, transactional per migration.
    73	- **Export:** `dump(out: &mut dyn Write, format: DumpFormat) -> Result<DumpStats>`.
    74	
    75	**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D6, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
    76	
    77	## 4. Key encoding
    78	
    79	Full byte layout for primary + every secondary in [`lmdb/keys.md`](lmdb/keys.md). At a glance:
    80	
    81	- Primary `events`: `event_id[32]` → `Event` (CBOR via `nostr` crate's serialization). Owned by `nostr-lmdb`.
    82	- Secondary `idx_author_kind`: `pubkey[32] || kind_be[4] || created_at_desc_be[8] || event_id[32]` → empty. NMP-owned. `created_at_desc_be = (u64::MAX - created_at).to_be_bytes()` for newest-first forward scans.
    83	- Secondary `idx_kind_dtag`: `kind_be[4] || pubkey[32] || dtag_len_be[2] || dtag_bytes` → `event_id[32]`. NMP-owned. Parameterized replaceable exact-key lookup (by author).
    84	- Secondary `idx_kind_dtag_time`: `kind_be[4] || dtag_len_be[2] || dtag_bytes || created_at_desc_be[8] || event_id[32]` → empty. NMP-owned. Newest-first scan across all authors for a `(kind, d_tag)` pair; used by `scan_by_kind_dtag`.
    85	- Secondary `idx_etag_time`, `idx_ptag_time`: `tag_value[32] || created_at_desc_be[8] || event_id[32]` → `kind_be[4]`. NMP-owned. Value holds kind so reaction views can filter without a primary fetch.
    86	- Secondary `idx_kind_time`: `kind_be[4] || created_at_desc_be[8] || event_id[32]` → empty.
    87	- Secondary `idx_expires`: `expires_at_be[8] || event_id[32]` → empty. Scanned by the NIP-40 reaper.
    88	- `tombstones`: `target_id[32]` → CBOR `TombstoneRow { kind5_event_id: Option<[u8;32]>, origin: TombstoneOrigin, deleter_pubkey: Option<[u8;32]>, deleted_at, sources: Vec<RelayUrl> }`.
    89	- `tombstones_addr`: `pubkey[32] || kind_be[4] || dtag_len_be[2] || dtag_bytes` → CBOR `TombstoneRow`. For kind:5 `a`-tag deletes arriving before the target event exists (see [`lmdb/keys.md`](lmdb/keys.md) §4.2).
    90	
    91	All integer fields in keys are big-endian so LMDB's byte-wise comparator matches numeric order. `created_at_desc_be = (u64::MAX - created_at).to_be_bytes()` produces newest-first forward scans without `MDB_LAST + MDB_PREV`.
    92	
    93	All secondaries are maintained inside the same `RwTxn` as the primary write — atomicity is achieved by LMDB transactionality, not by post-hoc reconciliation. This requires NMP and `nostr-lmdb` to share a single `lmdb::Environment`; the decision is in ADR-0011 (`docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md`).
    94	
    95	## 5. Watermark table

 succeeded in 0ms:
   128	    // ─────── Hot-set / claims (GC) ───────
   129	
   130	    /// Register the maximum number of events this view is allowed to pin at once.
   131	    /// Must be called before `claim()` for a given `claimer`. If not called,
   132	    /// the store applies a default per-view ceiling of `max_claim_per_view` events
   133	    /// (see [`gc.md`](gc.md) §2 — default 1 000).
   134	    ///
   135	    /// Enforcement: `claim()` counts the current per-claimer set size; if adding
   136	    /// `ids` would exceed this budget OR the global `max_pinned_total` ceiling,
   137	    /// it returns `Err(StoreError::OverPinned { ... })` without modifying state.
   138	    /// The caller is responsible for releasing stale claims first.
   139	    ///
   140	    /// Rationale: D8 (reactivity contract) requires that the kernel's working-set
   141	    /// is bounded at all times. An unbounded pin overlay would let a misbehaving
   142	    /// view module inflate memory without limit (ADR-0001..0004).
   143	    fn register_view_cover(&self, claimer: ClaimerId, cover_budget: usize) -> Result<(), StoreError>;
   144	
   145	    /// Pin `ids` against eviction until `release()`. Returns `StoreError::OverPinned`
   146	    /// if adding `ids` would exceed the per-claimer budget or the global ceiling.
   147	    fn claim(&self, claimer: ClaimerId, ids: &[EventId]) -> Result<(), StoreError>;
   148	    fn release(&self, claimer: ClaimerId) -> Result<(), StoreError>;
   149	
   150	    /// Soft hint: keep these in hot LRU on a best-effort basis.
   151	    fn hot_set_hint(&self, ids: &[EventId]) -> Result<(), StoreError>;
   152	
   153	    /// One bounded GC pass — reap expired, trim LRU, purge old tombstones.
   154	    fn gc_step(&self, budget: GcBudget) -> Result<GcReport, StoreError>;
   155	
   156	    // ─────── Domain rows (per-DomainModule typed namespace) ───────
   157	
   158	    fn domain_open(&self, namespace: &'static str) -> Result<DomainHandle<'_>, StoreError>;
   159	    fn run_migrations(&self, namespace: &'static str, target_version: u32,
   160	                      migrations: &[crate::substrate::DomainMigration])
   161	        -> Result<(), StoreError>;
   162	
   163	    // ─────── Export ───────
   164	
   165	    fn dump(&self, out: &mut dyn std::io::Write, format: DumpFormat)
   166	        -> Result<DumpStats, StoreError>;
   167	}
   168	```
   169	
   170	`DeleteFilter` mirrors the limited subset of admin operations the kernel needs (by-relay-only events, by-author, by-id-list, by-kind range); it is **not** a pass-through to `nostr::Filter` — we intentionally do not expose arbitrary remote filters as a delete vector.
   171	
   172	`Coverage` (returned by `coverage()`):
   173	
   174	```rust
   175	pub enum Coverage {
   176	    CompleteAsOf(u64),  // fully synced; a cache miss is authoritative "doesn't exist"
   177	    PartialUpTo(u64),   // synced up to timestamp but row is stale — fetch is needed
   178	    Unknown,            // no watermark; always fetch
   179	}
   180	```
   181	
   182	## 3. `DomainHandle`
   183	
   184	```rust
   185	pub struct DomainHandle<'env> {
   186	    pub(crate) namespace: &'static str,
   187	    pub(crate) inner: DomainHandleInner<'env>,  // backend-specific
   188	}
   189	
   190	impl<'env> DomainHandle<'env> {
   191	    pub fn put(&self, key: &[u8], value: &[u8]) -> Result<(), StoreError>;
   192	    pub fn get(&self, key: &[u8]) -> Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, StoreError>;
   193	    pub fn delete(&self, key: &[u8]) -> Result<bool, StoreError>;
   194	    pub fn scan_prefix<'a>(&'a self, prefix: &[u8])
   195	        -> Result<Box<dyn Iterator<Item = Result<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>), StoreError>> + 'a>, StoreError>;
   196	    pub fn scan_index<'a>(&'a self, index: &'static str, key_prefix: &[u8])
   197	        -> Result<Box<dyn Iterator<Item = Result<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>), StoreError>> + 'a>, StoreError>;
   198	}
   199	```
   200	
   201	A handle is module-scoped; the kernel does not give a `DraftsModule` handle to `SettingsModule` (per `kernel-substrate.md` §8 "Domain stores are isolated"). The handle is `'env`-bounded so it cannot outlive the LMDB environment.
   202	
   203	## 4. Error semantics (doctrine D6)
   204	
   205	The trait returns `Result<T, StoreError>`. The actor's wrapper functions map them as:
   206	
   207	- `Io / Corrupt` at startup → panic (we cannot run without a store; surfaces to platform shell as a process restart).
   208	- `Io / Corrupt` mid-run → `Effect::StoreDegraded { details }` published on the diagnostics bridge (ADR-0007); the affected operation returns the closest-fit graceful default (empty iterator, drop-write); the next gc_step retries.
   209	- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")`.
   210	- `SchemaTooNew` at startup → publish `Effect::DomainSchemaTooNew { namespace }`, the affected module starts in degraded mode (its actions reject with `ActionRejection::ModuleUnavailable`), rest of the kernel runs.
   211	- `MigrationFailed` → same as above, plus a one-time toast on first action attempt.
   212	- `UnknownNamespace` → programming error; assert in debug, log + drop in release.
   213	- `OverPinned` → the caller (actor) surfaces this as `Effect::ViewOverPinned { claimer }` and then calls `release(claimer)` to drop the offending claim, keeping the working set bounded per D8.
   214	
   215	No `StoreError` ever crosses FFI (D6). The `AppUpdate` carries only successful state + optional `toast: Option<String>`.
   216	
   217	## 5. Two backends in v1
   218	
   219	```rust
   220	// In-memory backend, kept for tests + web-pre-M15.

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/design/lmdb-schema.md | sed -n '140,160p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a1e976d43e1097718
 succeeded in 0ms:
   140	`hot_resident` lives in a `lru::LruCache<EventId, Arc<Event>>` capped at the configured hot ceiling (default 10,000) plus a bounded pinned overlay holding events with non-zero claim count. The pinned overlay has two ceilings: per-view (`max_claim_per_view`, default 1 000) and global (`max_pinned_total`, default 20 000). `claim()` returns `StoreError::OverPinned` if either ceiling would be breached; see [`lmdb/gc.md`](lmdb/gc.md) §2 for enforcement details. `cold` lives only on disk; lookup pays one LMDB `get` (memory-mapped — typically already in OS page cache for recently-evicted items).
   141	
   142	**Eviction algorithm.** On any insert that pushes the LRU over its ceiling, the oldest non-pinned entry is dropped. `gc_step()` is called periodically by the actor (default every 60 s and on memory pressure callbacks from `MemoryWarningCapability`): it (a) reaps NIP-40 expired events using `idx_expires`, (b) trims the LRU to `target_hot_size`, (c) deletes tombstones older than `tombstone_retention` (default 90 days) whose target event is absent from the store, (d) returns a `GcReport` for diagnostics.
   143	
   144	ADR-0003's numbers are preserved as the M3 exit gate (§11 below): ≤ 100 MB working-set at 100 active views / 10k hot events / 1M cached on disk.
   145	
   146	## 8. Replaceable + tombstone semantics
   147	
   148	The `insert()` path implements exactly the §7.1 invariants:
   149	
   150	- **Replaceable (kinds 0, 3, 10000–19999).** Look up the existing event for `(pubkey, kind)` in `idx_author_kind` (most recent suffix). If incoming `created_at` is newer, replace; if equal, keep lexicographically smallest `id`; else drop. Replacement deletes the old primary row and all secondary entries in the same `RwTxn`.
   151	- **Parameterized replaceable (30000–39999).** Same algorithm keyed on `(pubkey, kind, d-tag)` via `idx_kind_dtag` (which holds `event_id` as value so we don't need a separate `idx_author_kind_dtag`; the dtag prefix is unique per author by Nostr semantics — see [`lmdb/keys.md`](lmdb/keys.md) §3.2 for the per-author scoping note).
   152	- **Kind:5 self-delete.** Verify signature, scan referenced `e` and `a` tags.
   153	  - For `e` tags: if the target event exists in the store and was authored by the deleter, delete the primary + all secondaries + write an event-id tombstone row. Tombstone timestamp = `max(existing.deleted_at, kind5.created_at)`. Re-insert of the deleted event id is suppressed by a `tombstones.contains(event_id)` check at insert time, returning `InsertOutcome::Tombstoned { id, kind5_event_id: Some(kind5.id), origin: Kind5 }`.
   154	  - For `a` tags (address format `<kind>:<pubkey>:<d-tag>`): if the target parameterized replaceable exists, same as `e` tag path. If it does **not** yet exist, write an address tombstone in `tombstones_addr` keyed by `(pubkey, kind, d-tag)`. When the target event is later inserted, the address tombstone match suppresses it and promotes an event-id tombstone for fast future lookups (see [`lmdb/keys.md`](lmdb/keys.md) §4.2).
   155	- **Foreign kind:5.** A kind:5 referencing events not authored by the kind:5's `pubkey` is ignored (per spec) — the event is *still stored* as a kind:5 (so other clients can render it / dedup it), but it has no side effect on the targets. The tombstone row is **not** written.
   156	- **NIP-40 expiration.** On insert, parse `expiration` tag; if present, write `idx_expires`. On `gc_step()`, scan `idx_expires` for keys with `expires_at_be ≤ now`, delete them like kind:5 (full primary + secondaries + tombstone marker noting `kind: Expired`).
   157	
   158	The tombstone schema is in [`lmdb/keys.md`](lmdb/keys.md) §4.
   159	
   160	## 9. Provenance: per-row sidecar sub-database

./README.md:29:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D8).** Canonical wording from [`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5](docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected. Conflicts resolve in the order listed. **Two kinds:** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime / FFI / hot-path constraints). Both equally binding.
./README.md:33:  - **D3** Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out. (M2.)
./README.md:36:  - **D6** Errors never cross FFI as exceptions. Surface as `toast: Option<String>` state fields. (RMP bible invariant #2.)
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:7:**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:40:## D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:61:## D6. Errors never cross FFI as exceptions

docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:Summary: D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime implementation constraints). Both are equally binding. Every API decision answers to at least one; conflicts resolve in the order D0 > D1 > … > D8.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:38:| D3 | Outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:| D6 | Errors never cross FFI as exceptions | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:123:    /// set is a policy choice that lives inside lane 4 (User-configured). D3: the operator
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D6, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:62:The test's assertion that no plaintext nsec crosses FFI is a key-security invariant: the platform layer never sees unencrypted key material because every signing operation goes through the in-Rust `IdentityModule::sign` function. Signer failures surface as `toast: Option<String>` state (per **D6**, "errors never cross FFI as exceptions"; `overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D6) rather than as exceptions or `Result<T, E>` across the FFI boundary.
docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:71:| Error strings in JSON (D3) | Design compliance | D3-compliant: errors as advisory data, not FFI codes. |
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:203:## 4. Error semantics (doctrine D6)
docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:215:No `StoreError` ever crosses FFI (D6). The `AppUpdate` carries only successful state + optional `toast: Option<String>`.

docs/design/framework-magic/kind3.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the canonical NDK-era bug: app code listens for kind:3 events, manually closes its open subscriptions, re-derives author lists, re-issues REQs, and either races itself (REQ ordering vs. local-state ordering) or leaks the old REQ. This contract structurally forbids that pattern: the view module never sees the kind:3 directly, and the app never issues a REQ. Specifically discharges **D3** (outbox routing automatic; see `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D3) and the auto-group/auto-close property of **C8** for the follow-list-change case.
docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:280:- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:31:| **D6** errors never cross FFI as exceptions | C7 (publish fail → `PublishPlanError` state, not exception); C11 (signer errors → ledger state); implicitly all 13 via the actor's toast-surface design |

./docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:46:- **D6–D8: substrate invariants** — runtime / FFI / hot-path constraints (errors-never-FFI, capabilities-report, reactivity-contract).

docs/design/lmdb/tests/insert.md:18:Plus a runtime-instrumented test enforcing that `insert()` is the only path that writes to the primary `events` sub-db. The test uses a `WriteCounting<S>` newtype that wraps any `EventStore` and intercepts every write; at teardown it asserts that all writes to the primary sub-db originated from `insert()` call frames (verified via a `AtomicBool` flag set on entry to `insert` and checked inside the write interceptor).

./docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D8): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 negentropy first, REQ second · D3 outbox routing automatic, manual relay is the opt-out · D4 single writer per fact; caches derive · D5 snapshots bounded by open views · D6 errors never cross FFI as exceptions · D7 capabilities report, never decide policy · D8 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60 Hz/view, working-set bounded). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4600:++**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5633:++## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5639:++- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6347:+docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:65:| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6386:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6416:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6417:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1165:+- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6438:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:15:You are reviewing merge 0dfb97581315aa04bd66341a98a32215de5e14d6 (the M11 podcast-app rebuild design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6447:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:993:+| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6451:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2087:+- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6696:+docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

./docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:123:    /// set is a policy choice that lives inside lane 4 (User-configured). D3: the operator
./docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:17:Per doctrine D6: "errors never cross FFI as exceptions." The corollary is that the *store itself* must never silently produce an incorrect answer because of partial writes. Any scenario where two separate `lmdb::Environment` handles could each commit independently would violate this — we would have no way to roll back one side when the other fails.
./docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:19:**Why this matters (D6):** if the store returns wrong data because of a partial write, that wrong data eventually reaches FFI as an `AppUpdate`. There is no way to signal "this snapshot is partially corrupt" through a `Result<T, StoreError>` once the data is already in the `AppUpdate` struct. The only safe design is to prevent partial writes at the store layer.

./docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:12:Applesauce action can pass any relay list. NMP's structural guarantee is stronger — D3
./docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:78:    /// Does NOT fall back to indexers (D3) — publishes must reach only declared write relays.
./docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:107:    // intentionally no `indexer_set` field: publish NEVER falls back to indexers (D3).
./docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:109:    // indexer the author has not declared as a write relay violates D3 (outbox routing
./docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:184:- **Step 2's `resolve_author_outbox_no_indexer`** is the structural enforcement of D3 and
./docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:289:- The **author's own write relays** receive the event (standard D3 outbox rule).
./docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:62:The test's assertion that no plaintext nsec crosses FFI is a key-security invariant: the platform layer never sees unencrypted key material because every signing operation goes through the in-Rust `IdentityModule::sign` function. Signer failures surface as `toast: Option<String>` state (per **D6**, "errors never cross FFI as exceptions"; `overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D6) rather than as exceptions or `Result<T, E>` across the FFI boundary.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:811:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. ./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2628:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2482:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1911:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6677:+docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:258:| D3 | PASS | S7 metrics.json + toast-bridge merge SHA | <name> | <date> |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2631:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2485:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1914:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6696:+docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2635:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2489:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1918:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6711:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2648:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2502:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1931:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6781:+    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2653:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2507:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1936:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7096:+    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2654:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2508:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1937:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7153:+     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2663:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2517:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1946:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7398:+    75	**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2932:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2786:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2216:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3815:    62	The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2933:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2787:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2217:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3860:    34	The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3008:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2862:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2559:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:281:| D3 | PASS | S7 metrics.json + toast-bridge merge SHA | <name> | <date> |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3015:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2871:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3019:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2875:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3414:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3270:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1873:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:65:| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3419:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3275:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1912:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3420:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3276:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1942:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3421:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3277:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1943:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1165:+- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3426:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3282:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1964:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:15:You are reviewing merge 0dfb97581315aa04bd66341a98a32215de5e14d6 (the M11 podcast-app rebuild design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.

No business logic. |

Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3593:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3449:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2191:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:211:  note that this milestone *closes* the D3-incomplete state
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3602:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3458:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2203:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:258:| D3 | PASS | S7 metrics.json + toast-bridge merge SHA | <name> | <date> |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3606:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3462:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2222:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3610:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3466:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2237:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3628:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3484:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2307:    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3635:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3491:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2622:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3639:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3495:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2679:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:292:++| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:293:++| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:462:++| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:614:++Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:662:++## The two bullets together discharge D3
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:664:++C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:670:++The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:966:++The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5681:+- [README.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/README.md:25) vs [docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54): D0-D5 are inconsistent. README says D2 reactivity, D3 errors-never-FFI, D5 capabilities-report; product spec and framework-magic use D2 negentropy, D3 outbox, D5 snapshots. Fix one canonical doctrine map, then update framework-magic citations.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5704:+- [README.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/README.md:25) vs [docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54): D0-D5 are inconsistent. ./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5734:+**Why:** Codex review of merge `51120cb` flagged that I had been using a different D2/D3/D5 mapping throughout this session — conflating the product-spec D0–D5 with RMP-bible invariants (errors-never-cross-FFI is invariant #2; capabilities-report-don't-decide is RMP cardinal rule #6; ≤60 Hz reactivity is from ADR-0001..0004). They're all real binding rules, but they're three different rule sources. I had collapsed them into a single "D0–D5" rubric, which was wrong.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5887:    15	**Why:** Codex review of merge `51120cb` flagged that I had been using a different D2/D3/D5 mapping throughout this session — conflating the product-spec D0–D5 with RMP-bible invariants (errors-never-cross-FFI is invariant #2; capabilities-report-don't-decide is RMP cardinal rule #6; ≤60 Hz reactivity is from ADR-0001..0004). They're all real binding rules, but they're three different rule sources. I had collapsed them into a single "D0–D5" rubric, which was wrong.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7381:+**Why:** Codex review of merge `51120cb` flagged that I had been using a different D2/D3/D5 mapping throughout this session — conflating the product-spec D0–D5 with RMP-bible invariants (errors-never-cross-FFI is invariant #2; capabilities-report-don't-decide is RMP cardinal rule #6; ≤60 Hz reactivity is from ADR-0001..0004). They're all real binding rules, but they're three different rule sources. I had collapsed them into a single "D0–D5" rubric, which was wrong.
./docs/design/framework-magic/kind3.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the canonical NDK-era bug: app code listens for kind:3 events, manually closes its open subscriptions, re-derives author lists, re-issues REQs, and either races itself (REQ ordering vs. local-state ordering) or leaks the old REQ. This contract structurally forbids that pattern: the view module never sees the kind:3 directly, and the app never issues a REQ. Specifically discharges **D3** (outbox routing automatic; see `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D3) and the auto-group/auto-close property of **C8** for the follow-list-change case.
./docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
./docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:31:| **D6** errors never cross FFI as exceptions | C7 (publish fail → `PublishPlanError` state, not exception); C11 (signer errors → ledger state); implicitly all 13 via the actor's toast-surface design |
./docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:227:docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:195:### D3: Errors Never Cross FFI — COMPLIANT
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:232:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:15:**Why:** Codex review of merge `51120cb` flagged that I had been using a different D2/D3/D5 mapping throughout this session — conflating the product-spec D0–D5 with RMP-bible invariants (errors-never-cross-FFI is invariant #2; capabilities-report-don't-decide is RMP cardinal rule #6; ≤60 Hz reactivity is from ADR-0001..0004). They're all real binding rules, but they're three different rule sources. I had collapsed them into a single "D0–D5" rubric, which was wrong.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:239:ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/SharedViews.swift:149:        "FF453A", "BF5AF2", "64D2FF", "FFD60A",
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:247:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:293:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:19:- D3: outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:323:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:81:The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:327:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:547:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2245:docs/design/framework-magic.md:35:| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:548:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2246:docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:577:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2280:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:619:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2356:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:677:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2455:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:697:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2475:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:710:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2488:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:711:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2489:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:712:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2490:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2039:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1467:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3700:+    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2053:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1481:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4600:++**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2054:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1483:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5633:++## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2055:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1484:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5639:++- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2070:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1499:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5900:+docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2077:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1506:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5915:+docs/plan.md:356:  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).

No business logic. |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2359:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1788:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6386:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2485:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1914:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6696:+docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2489:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1918:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6711:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2502:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1931:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6781:+    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2507:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1936:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7096:+    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2508:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1937:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7153:+     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2517:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1946:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7398:+    75	**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2519:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1949:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7885:+   282	## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2871:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2875:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2876:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1165:+- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
