./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2897:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1426:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2907:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1441:docs/plan.md:356:  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2910:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1448:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:15:You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity contract, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). ./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3275:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1912:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3462:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2222:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3785:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3793:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3820:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:65:| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3918:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3951:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3966:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3967:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3968:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3973:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:282:## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3974:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:288:- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4029:    29	  - **D3** Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out. Reads route per author write relays; publishes route to author write relays + tagged recipients' inbox relays. (See M2.)
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4144:    29	  - **D3** Errors never cross FFI — become `toast: Option<String>` state fields
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4260:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4376:    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4474:The main discrepancy is now clear: 810d0f8’s README used the older “RMP invariant” labels as D2/D3/D5, while the product spec’s canonical §1.5 uses negentropy/outbox/snapshots. I’m doing one more pass over status and path claims so the final review stays focused.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4661:    34	| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:322:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. ./docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:771:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:282:## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:772:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:288:- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:773:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:775:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:776:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:777:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:779:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:786:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:804:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:905:There’s a larger consistency issue around the doctrine names: the plan header still names the old D2/D3/D5 meanings, while the newer framework-magic docs and your review brief use negentropy/outbox/bounded-snapshots. I’m checking whether that is isolated to the plan header or repeated in the touched docs.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1224:docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:1289:- `docs/plan.md:9` misstates the cardinal doctrine. It replaces canonical D2/D3/D5 with “reactivity contract,” “errors never cross FFI,” and “capabilities report.” Fix to match `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54-79`: D2 negentropy first, D3 automatic outbox routing, D5 snapshots bounded by open views. Also relabel `docs/plan.md:356`; FFI error shape is not D3.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4261:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:223:+| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4262:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:224:+| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4263:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:225:+| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4289:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:394:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4300:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:546:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4302:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:594:+## The two bullets together discharge D3
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4303:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:596:+C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4304:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:602:+The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4328:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:898:+The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4456:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4326:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5118:docs/perf/codex-reviews/151c80c.md:19:- D3: outbox routing automatic
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5126:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5138:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5139:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1165:+- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5476:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1873:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:65:| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5481:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1912:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5483:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1942:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5489:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1964:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:15:You are reviewing merge 0dfb97581315aa04bd66341a98a32215de5e14d6 (the M11 podcast-app rebuild design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5622:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2222:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

Hex-validated via `is_hex_pubkey`. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace. Empty / non-hex inputs are **silently dropped** (see §7 finding D3-gap). | Silent no-op on null app or null pubkey. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1007:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:225:+| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1010:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:394:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1012:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:546:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1569:docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:71:| Error strings in JSON (D3) | Design compliance | D3-compliant: errors as advisory data, not FFI codes. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1576:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:18:  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1582:docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory-findings.md:195:### D3: Errors Never Cross FFI — COMPLIANT
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1589:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:15:**Why:** Codex review of merge `51120cb` flagged that I had been using a different D2/D3/D5 mapping throughout this session — conflating the product-spec D0–D5 with RMP-bible invariants (errors-never-cross-FFI is invariant #2; capabilities-report-don't-decide is RMP cardinal rule #6; ≤60 Hz reactivity is from ADR-0001..0004). They're all real binding rules, but they're three different rule sources. I had collapsed them into a single "D0–D5" rubric, which was wrong.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1608:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:280:- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1622:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:65:| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1643:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1668:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:282:## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1669:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:288:- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1678:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1691:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1694:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1696:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1697:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1698:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1711:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1742:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:81:The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1802:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1794:+docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1822:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2111:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1852:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3700:+    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1900:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4600:++**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1912:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5633:++## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1913:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5639:++- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:2250:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6347:+docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:65:| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:2255:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6386:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:2257:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6416:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)

File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:2674:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3043:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1012:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:546:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3308:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:66:| 8 | `nmp_app_open_author(*mut, *const c_char)` | `void nmp_app_open_author(void *app, const char *pubkey)` | UTF-8 C string, expected 64-char lowercase hex pubkey. Hex-validated via `is_hex_pubkey`. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace. Empty / non-hex inputs are **silently dropped** (see §7 finding D3-gap). | Silent no-op on null app or null pubkey. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3313:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:120:| S7 | Error-shape exhaustion | every invalid input path | D3 (no errors cross FFI) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3317:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:196:| D3-doc | `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/status.rs::relay_status_for` | Doc that `last_error`/`last_notice` are advisory data fields (D3-compliant: errors as state, not as FFI returns) | 3 min |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3318:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:214:### 7.2 D3 structural gap (named, not hidden)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3319:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:220:caller and without setting any state field. This is **D3-compliant in
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3320:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:222:D3-incomplete in the user-visible sense** (no toast surfaces in
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3321:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:225:The debt-inventory's D3 audit (lines 317–334) concludes the same:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3328:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:250:| **D3** errors never cross FFI | S7 (exhaustion) + §7.2 (toast bridge) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3339:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3358:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:282:## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3359:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:288:- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3505:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:192:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3510:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:197:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:15:**Why:** Codex review of merge `51120cb` flagged that I had been using a different D2/D3/D5 mapping throughout this session — conflating the product-spec D0–D5 with RMP-bible invariants (errors-never-cross-FFI is invariant #2; capabilities-report-don't-decide is RMP cardinal rule #6; ≤60 Hz reactivity is from ADR-0001..0004). They're all real binding rules, but they're three different rule sources. I had collapsed them into a single "D0–D5" rubric, which was wrong.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3518:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:205:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3526:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:215:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:280:- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3527:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:216:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3851:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1911:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6677:+docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:258:| D3 | PASS | S7 metrics.json + toast-bridge merge SHA | <name> | <date> |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3854:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1914:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6696:+docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3858:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1918:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6711:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3999:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2065:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8780:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:156:> the spec **is exactly six items: D0, D1, D2, D3, D4, D5.** This
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4028:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2094:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4029:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2095:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4030:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2096:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4031:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2097:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4034:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2102:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:17:NMP doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns; D1 best-effort placeholders; D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view + working-set bound; D3 errors never cross FFI; D4 one writer per fact; D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4039:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2108:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:103:+  - **D3** Errors never cross FFI — become `toast: Option<String>` state fields
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4045:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2115:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:223:+| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4046:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2116:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:224:+| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4047:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2117:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:225:+| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4060:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2130:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:394:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4064:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2134:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:546:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4065:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2135:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:594:+## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4066:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2136:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:596:+C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4067:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2137:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:602:+The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4069:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2139:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:898:+The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4430:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3809:    56	C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4431:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3815:    62	The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4433:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3860:    34	The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4445:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4051:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4448:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4065:    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4456:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4326:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:4492:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5613:- [README.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/README.md:25) vs [docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54): D0-D5 are inconsistent. README says D2 reactivity, D3 errors-never-FFI, D5 capabilities-report; product spec and framework-magic use D2 negentropy, D3 outbox, D5 snapshots. Fix one canonical doctrine map, then update framework-magic citations.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5027:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1777:docs/plan.md:356:  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5033:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1982:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5040:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2067:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5070:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3656:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5118:docs/perf/codex-reviews/151c80c.md:19:- D3: outbox routing automatic
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5126:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5138:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5139:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1165:+- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5445:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1824:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3990:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5448:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1827:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4005:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5476:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1873:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:65:| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5481:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1912:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5483:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1942:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5489:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1964:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:15:You are reviewing merge 0dfb97581315aa04bd66341a98a32215de5e14d6 (the M11 podcast-app rebuild design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5622:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2222:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2193:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:81:The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2244:docs/design/framework-magic.md:34:| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2245:docs/design/framework-magic.md:35:| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2246:docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2280:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2356:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2455:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2475:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2488:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2489:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2490:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3402:docs/design/framework-magic.md:35:| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3403:docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:4093:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.

./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2490:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3402:docs/design/framework-magic.md:35:| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3403:docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:4093:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:254:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:406:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:454:+## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:456:+C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:462:+The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:758:+The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:748:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:13:| 2026-05-18 03:25 | 5 | Heartbeat #5 (autonomous). T10 plan-splitter landed ab632c1 + b49f8f3 (plan.md split into 25 per-milestone files + scope folded; codex follow-up clean). T11 codex-fixer-2 final commit (446a212) landed. T12 clippy-cleaner finished (1e358e1.md review clean, 8 lints fixed). T17 framework-magic-designer transcribed already landed. Codex review of cumulative HB4 diff flagged 6 substantive issues, biggest being a DOCTRINE VOCABULARY COLLISION I had introduced — D2/D3/D5 wording was inconsistent across README + framework-magic vs canonical product-spec/overview-and-dx.md §1.5 (which has D2 negentropy / D3 outbox / D5 snapshots-bounded). Logged as PD-001 in pending-user-decisions.md; README aligned in 3f5a2b3; T19 dispatched to align framework-magic docs. Also dispatched T20 (catch up missing codex review commits to make README claim "codex on every merge" true). Also dispatched T21 (research NDK svelte $metaSubscribe per user directive — Opus). T13 (M2 fix) dispatched this tick. Shell drift bug: pwd had silently switched into m1-hardener's worktree dir at start of heartbeat — recovered with explicit cd. Worktree cleanups: 5 completed workers (a114ff4e9/a983d7768/a9713e6e/a9c828b0/afbb948b) removed + T10 (a1da29d) removed. DerivedData 5.9G, disk 95% — within tolerance, no purge needed. 6 agents in flight: m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, framework-magic-reconciler, codex-review-catchup, meta-subscribe-analyst, m2-codex-fixer. Pending T14 (M3 fix) held until queue clears. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:749:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:14:| 2026-05-18 03:45 | 6 | Heartbeat #6 (autonomous). MAJOR: T1 m1-hardener landed 701d0e5 — M1 PASSES empirically. Live firehose-bench cold_start 258ms first-item / 3587ms filled-timeline; profile_thrashing 0 leaked subs over 10min. M1 ✅ DONE. Two deferrals named (memory RSS → M10.5, dispatch-rate platform gate → M14) tracked as T22. T21 meta-subscribe-analyst landed b0ae439 (substrate-fit analysis: build as reference view module, ~30 LOC compiler change for address-pointer support); two own follow-up commits 5b86de4 + 6782b97 codex-cleaned. T13 m2-codex-fixer landed 5ff0ac5 (M2 design fixes round 1). User landed PD-001 RESOLVED option (b): expanded canonical doctrine D0-D5 → D0-D8 in product-spec/overview-and-dx.md §1.5 (added D6 errors-never-FFI / D7 capabilities-report / D8 reactivity-contract; two-kind classification: D0-D5 policy / D6-D8 substrate). README aligned in ea3d40e + aef42c9. Codex review of cumulative HB5 wave (b0ae439.md) flagged 5 issues — biggest is D0 violation: public ActorCommand exposes domain nouns (OpenAuthor/ClaimProfile/etc.) from nmp-core. Tracked as T23. Worktree cleanups: T1 (ad49d31a), T21 (a5a7f37a), T13 (a67512cc) removed. T22 + T23 dispatched this tick. Pending: none. 5 agents in flight: T14 m3-codex-fixer, T18 highlighter-explorer, T19 framework-magic-reconciler, T22 m1-deferral-folder, T23 m1-codex-fixer. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:811:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2631:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2485:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1914:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6696:+docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3008:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2862:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2559:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:281:| D3 | PASS | S7 metrics.json + toast-bridge merge SHA | <name> | <date> |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3015:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2871:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3019:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2875:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3041:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2897:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1426:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3051:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2907:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1441:docs/plan.md:356:  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3177:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3033:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1606:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2361:++> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3381:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3237:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1821:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3985:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3384:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3240:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1824:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3990:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3387:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3243:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1827:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4005:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3414:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3270:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1873:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:65:| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3419:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3275:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1912:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3420:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3276:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1942:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3421:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3277:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1943:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1165:+- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3426:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3282:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1964:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:15:You are reviewing merge 0dfb97581315aa04bd66341a98a32215de5e14d6 (the M11 podcast-app rebuild design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.

./docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
./docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:Summary: D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime implementation constraints). Both are equally binding. Every API decision answers to at least one; conflicts resolve in the order D0 > D1 > … > D8.
./docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:38:| D3 | Outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out | Policy |
./docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:| D6 | Errors never cross FFI as exceptions | Substrate |
./docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:7:**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."
./docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:40:## D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
./docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:61:## D6. Errors never cross FFI as exceptions

 succeeded in 0ms:
commit 85770db30d9527b2bb64e5f1e99d648cd7634e3d
Author:     pablof7z <pfer@me.com>
AuthorDate: Mon May 18 03:15:16 2026 +0300
Commit:     pablof7z <pfer@me.com>
CommitDate: Mon May 18 03:15:23 2026 +0300

    docs(m3): codex follow-up — scan ordering + claim set + D6 cite + signature align
    
    - #1 (HIGH): add idx_kind_dtag_time (kind|dtag|created_at_desc|event_id → empty)
      for newest-first (kind, d_tag) scans across all authors; fix scan_by_kind_dtag()
      signature in EventStore trait (was wrong pubkey-based scan; now d_tag + since/until);
      add idx_kind_dtag_time §3.3 to keys.md; update lmdb-schema.md secondary table.
    - #2 (MED): add LmdbEventStore::with_write_observer(Arc<dyn WriteObserver>) as a
      backend-internal test hook (NOT on the trait); document in tests.md §1.1.
    - #3 (MED): change by_claimer from SmallVec to BTreeSet<EventId> so claim() is
      idempotent — re-claiming an already-pinned id does not double-increment refcount.
      Budget checks count only genuinely new ids (compatible with ceiling enforcement).
      Update gc.md data structure + claim() logic + §4 idempotency prose.
      Update claims_meta value type in keys.md table.
    - #4 (MED): fix doctrine cite D3 → D6 in lmdb-schema.md:75.
    - #5 (LOW): drop now_s param from coverage() in watermarks.md §1.1; backend uses
      internal clock; return type aligned to Result<Coverage, StoreError>.
    - #6 (LOW): split tests.md (322 LOC → 184) into tests/insert.md (§2.1-2.7a, 123 LOC)
      and tests/migration.md (§2.12-2.13, 57 LOC); tests.md becomes index with table.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

30: docs/design/lmdb/tests.md               | 101 +++++++++++++++-
138:+- **Reads:** `get_by_id`, `scan_by_author_kind`, `get_param_replaceable`, `scan_by_kind_dtag`, `scan_by_etag`, `scan_by_ptag`, `scan_by_kind_time`, `scan_expiring_before`. All `scan_*` methods return a streaming `EventIter` so the planner pages without materialising. Cache-coverage queries via `coverage(WatermarkKey)` return a `Coverage` enum (`CompleteAsOf`, `PartialUpTo`, `Unknown`) used by the M2 planner.
147:-- Secondary `idx_kind_dtag`: `kind_be[4] || dtag_len_be[2] || dtag_bytes || pubkey[32]` → `event_id[32]`. NMP-owned. Parameterized replaceable address lookup.
150:+- Secondary `idx_kind_dtag`: `kind_be[4] || pubkey[32] || dtag_len_be[2] || dtag_bytes` → `event_id[32]`. NMP-owned. Parameterized replaceable address lookup. The `pubkey` field comes before `d-tag` bytes so all entries for a `(kind, pubkey)` pair are contiguous for `scan_by_kind_dtag`.
171:+`hot_resident` lives in a `lru::LruCache<EventId, Arc<Event>>` capped at the configured hot ceiling (default 10,000) plus a bounded pinned overlay holding events with non-zero claim count. The pinned overlay has two ceilings: per-view (`max_claim_per_view`, default 1 000) and global (`max_pinned_total`, default 20 000). `claim()` returns `StoreError::OverPinned` if either ceiling would be breached; see [`lmdb/gc.md`](lmdb/gc.md) §2 for enforcement details. `cold` lives only on disk; lookup pays one LMDB `get` (memory-mapped — typically already in OS page cache for recently-evicted items).
178: - **Parameterized replaceable (30000–39999).** Same algorithm keyed on `(pubkey, kind, d-tag)` via `idx_kind_dtag` (which holds `event_id` as value so we don't need a separate `idx_author_kind_dtag`; the dtag prefix is unique per author by Nostr semantics — see [`lmdb/keys.md`](lmdb/keys.md) §3.2 for the per-author scoping note).
202:     by_claimer: HashMap<ClaimerId, SmallVec<[EventId; 8]>>,
206:+    // Ceilings (enforced on every claim() call — D8 / ADR-0001..0004).
216:+    /// the lower ceiling applies to future claim() calls.
229:+        let current_for_claimer = self.by_claimer.get(&c).map_or(0, |v| v.len());
251:         self.by_claimer.entry(c).or_default().extend_from_slice(ids);
270:+            // trim() alone. The working-set budget enforcement in claim() is the
305: | Hot LRU (10k × Arc<Event>) | ~30 MB | average kind:1 event with content ~800 B, profile/contacts can be 4–8 KB each; mix-weighted average ~3 KB; the `Arc` is shared with view module payloads so the same body isn't duplicated |
306:-| Claim refcount maps (10k entries) | ~0.5 MB | `HashMap<EventId, u32>` + reverse `by_claimer` |
307:+| Claim refcount maps (≤20k pinned + 10k LRU entries) | ~1 MB | `HashMap<EventId, u32>` + reverse `by_claimer` + `view_budgets`; global ceiling 20k pins keeps this bounded |
314: | `release()` called for unknown `ClaimerId` | `by_claimer.remove` returns None | Silent no-op; logged at debug; not a bug (idempotent close) |
315:+| `claim()` exceeds per-view or global ceiling | Per-view: `by_claimer[c].len() + ids.len() > view_budgets[c]`; global: `pinned.len() + new_unique > max_pinned_total` | Return `StoreError::OverPinned`; state unchanged; actor surfaces `Effect::ViewOverPinned` and calls `release(claimer_id)` |

1178:One cross-doc mismatch is emerging: `trait.md` exposes `coverage(&WatermarkKey) -> Result<Coverage, StoreError>`, while `watermarks.md` still shows pseudocode taking `now_s`. I’m checking whether that is just explanatory pseudocode or a real contract conflict.

1241:    60	    pub sources: SmallVec<[ProvenanceEntry; 4]>,    // bounded at 32; see master doc §9
1257:    76	For a duplicate-id insert, the per-event provenance write is the **only** LMDB write (per [`keys.md`](keys.md) §9 worked example). The store reads the existing row, mutates the matching entry's `last_seen_ms` (or appends), re-sorts + recomputes `primary`, and writes it back. Total cost: 1 read + 1 write on a 4-element CBOR row — well under 50 µs on iPhone 12 NAND.

1680:    67	- **Reads:** `get_by_id`, `scan_by_author_kind`, `get_param_replaceable`, `scan_by_kind_dtag`, `scan_by_etag`, `scan_by_ptag`, `scan_by_kind_time`, `scan_expiring_before`. 1696:    83	- Secondary `idx_kind_dtag`: `kind_be[4] || pubkey[32] || dtag_len_be[2] || dtag_bytes` → `event_id[32]`. NMP-owned. Parameterized replaceable address lookup. The `pubkey` field comes before `d-tag` bytes so all entries for a `(kind, pubkey)` pair are contiguous for `scan_by_kind_dtag`.
1752:   139	`hot_resident` lives in a `lru::LruCache<EventId, Arc<Event>>` capped at the configured hot ceiling (default 10,000) plus a bounded pinned overlay holding events with non-zero claim count. The pinned overlay has two ceilings: per-view (`max_claim_per_view`, default 1 000) and global (`max_pinned_total`, default 20 000). `claim()` returns `StoreError::OverPinned` if either ceiling would be breached; see [`lmdb/gc.md`](lmdb/gc.md) §2 for enforcement details. `cold` lives only on disk; lookup pays one LMDB `get` (memory-mapped — typically already in OS page cache for recently-evicted items).
1763:   150	- **Parameterized replaceable (30000–39999).** Same algorithm keyed on `(pubkey, kind, d-tag)` via `idx_kind_dtag` (which holds `event_id` as value so we don't need a separate `idx_author_kind_dtag`; the dtag prefix is unique per author by Nostr semantics — see [`lmdb/keys.md`](lmdb/keys.md) §3.2 for the per-author scoping note).
1778:   165	On duplicate-id insert (§7.1 row 2), `insert()` does not touch the primary; it only updates the provenance sidecar (`last_seen_ms` bump on the matching `ProvenanceEntry`, or append). The "primary relay" — for outbox-routing scoring (M2) and ADR-0007 diagnostics — is deterministically the first relay observed (`sources[0]` after sort by `first_seen_ms`).

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/design/lmdb/gc.md | sed -n '180,255p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a1e976d43e1097718
 succeeded in 0ms:
   180	
   181	`gc_step()` is **never** invoked from an FFI call path — it runs on the actor's own schedule so any latency it introduces is invisible to the platform.
   182	
   183	## 4. Claim / release wiring
   184	
   185	The kernel actor holds `view_claims: HashMap<ViewId, ClaimerId>`. On `open_view(spec)`:
   186	
   187	1. The view module's `dependencies(spec)` is consulted (per `kernel-substrate.md` §3).
   188	2. The composite reverse-index resolves the dependency set to a (small, bounded) set of currently-known event ids — the *view cover*.
   189	3. `store.register_view_cover(claimer_id, cover_budget)` registers the budget ceiling for this view. `cover_budget` is `spec.max_cover_size()` (a per-view-module constant; defaults to 200 if unspecified).
   190	4. `store.claim(claimer_id, &cover_ids)` pins those events in hot. Returns `StoreError::OverPinned` if the registered budget is exceeded; the actor releases the claim and surfaces `Effect::ViewOverPinned`.
   191	5. As events arrive matching the dependency, the actor calls `store.claim(claimer_id, &[new_id])` incrementally. Because `by_claimer` uses `BTreeSet<EventId>`, re-claiming an already-pinned id is a no-op — the refcount in `pinned` is not double-incremented.
   192	
   193	On `close_view(view_id)`:
   194	
   195	1. `store.release(claimer_id)` drops every pin in one call.
   196	2. The view module's `state` is dropped; its claim refcounts decay; the next `gc_step()` evicts any newly-unpinned cold from LRU.
   197	
   198	Restart recovery: `claims_meta` sub-db ([`keys.md`](keys.md) §1) holds the persisted per-`ClaimerId` pin set. On startup the actor rebuilds active views first (per the diagnostics replay sequence), then re-claims; entries in `claims_meta` whose `ClaimerId` is not associated with a re-opened view are dropped from the persisted map. This means the cold-start path always re-derives claims from open-view state, but the persistence is what lets the store survive an actor restart without losing hot-set protection mid-shutdown.
   199	
   200	## 5. Memory accounting (the ADR-0003 gate)
   201	
   202	The relevant figure for the M3 exit gate is **working-set RSS at the configuration described in ADR-0003 §Decision**: 100 active views, 10k hot events, 1M cached on disk, ≤ 100 MB.
   203	
   204	Components measured:
   205	
   206	| Source | Approx bytes | Notes |
   207	|---|---|---|
   208	| Hot LRU (10k × Arc<Event>) | ~30 MB | average kind:1 event with content ~800 B, profile/contacts can be 4–8 KB each; mix-weighted average ~3 KB; the `Arc` is shared with view module payloads so the same body isn't duplicated |
   209	| Claim refcount maps (≤20k pinned + 10k LRU entries) | ~1 MB | `HashMap<EventId, u32>` + reverse `by_claimer: HashMap<ClaimerId, BTreeSet<EventId>>` + `view_budgets`; global ceiling 20k pins keeps this bounded |
   210	| Reverse index in-memory (composite keys for 100 views) | ~5 MB | from ADR-0001 — bounded by `~broad_axes_guardrail` per ADR-0001 |
   211	| Projection caches (author display, reaction counts) | ~10 MB | LRU-bounded by referenced-view count per ADR-0003 |
   212	| LMDB page cache (kernel-owned, *not* counted toward RSS budget) | 0 | OS-paged, evicted under pressure; counts against system memory but not app working set |
   213	| Watermarks (loaded as `HashMap` for hot lookups) | ~2 MB | M4 — assuming O(10k) watermarks (one per `(filter, relay)` pair) |
   214	| Tombstone bloom filter (if added — see open questions) | ~1 MB | accelerates the `tombstones.contains_key()` check on insert |
   215	| Action ledger in-flight rows | ~1 MB | bounded by spec §7.5 |
   216	| Slack / Rust allocator overhead | ~20 MB | empirical from reactivity-bench |
   217	| **Total target** | **~70 MB** | leaves ~30 MB headroom against the 100 MB gate |
   218	
   219	The 1M-events-on-disk dimension does **not** appear in the budget because LMDB does not page them into our heap; they exist in mmap'd pages the OS may evict at will. This is the design intent of ADR-0003.
   220	
   221	## 6. Failure modes and degraded behavior
   222	
   223	| Failure | Detection | Response |
   224	|---|---|---|
   225	| LMDB env out of space | LMDB `MDB_MAP_FULL` on a write | Run an emergency `gc_step()` with relaxed budget; if still full, surface `Effect::StoreOutOfSpace`, refuse new inserts, allow reads + deletes |
   226	| LRU evicted a still-pinned event (bug) | `trim()` would have skipped it; if observed, log + invariant violation | Pin reinstated from `claims_meta`; fire `tracing::error!`; flagged as critical bug class to investigate |
   227	| `gc_step()` over-budget | `start.elapsed() > max_duration_ms` mid-loop | Break out of current loop early; remaining work picked up next call (no state corruption — every reaped event is its own transaction) |
   228	| `release()` called for unknown `ClaimerId` | `by_claimer.remove` returns None | Silent no-op; logged at debug; not a bug (idempotent close) |
   229	| `claim()` exceeds per-view or global ceiling | Per-view: `by_claimer[c].len() + ids.len() > view_budgets[c]`; global: `pinned.len() + new_unique > max_pinned_total` | Return `StoreError::OverPinned`; state unchanged; actor surfaces `Effect::ViewOverPinned` and calls `release(claimer_id)` |
   230	| Memory warning during heavy insert burst | iOS `didReceiveMemoryWarning` → `MemoryWarningCapability` event | Actor lowers `target_hot_size` to 5k, runs `gc_step({max_events_per_step:5000, max_duration_ms:200})` once; restored after the warning clears |
   231	
   232	## 7. Diagnostics integration (ADR-0007)
   233	
   234	The store exposes a `StoreHealth` snapshot for the diagnostics bridge:
   235	
   236	```rust
   237	pub struct StoreHealth {
   238	    pub primary_event_count: u64,
   239	    pub tombstone_count: u64,
   240	    pub hot_lru_size: usize,
   241	    pub claim_pinned_count: usize,
   242	    pub watermark_count: usize,
   243	    pub on_disk_bytes: u64,
   244	    pub last_gc: Option<GcReport>,
   245	    pub last_gc_at_ms: Option<u128>,
   246	}
   247	```
   248	
   249	Surfaced in the diagnostics screen alongside relay status (ADR-0007 §1). The Phase 1a.7 proof app already has the rendering scaffold (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/DiagnosticsView.swift`); M3 adds the StoreHealth row to it.
   250	
   251	## 8. Why not a periodic full sweep?
   252	
   253	A full sweep is `O(stored_events)`. With 1M events on disk the LMDB scan alone is 100–500 ms wall-time on iPhone 12 NAND — well outside the actor's single-message budget. The bounded `gc_step()` with explicit budget is therefore the only correct shape; it composes with LMDB's natural mmap eviction model and never blocks the mailbox for long.
   254	
   255	A periodic vacuum/compact pass (LMDB's equivalent of `VACUUM`) **is** scheduled — once per app launch, at idle, after the first 30 seconds of quiescence. It is *not* part of `gc_step`'s budget envelope and runs as a separate low-priority actor message that yields between LMDB page boundaries.

- MED [docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:61](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a1e976d43e1097718/docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:61): `new_ids` is still a `Vec`, so `claim(c, &[id, id])` counts/increments twice before the `BTreeSet` can dedupe. [line 229](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a1e976d43e1097718/docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:229) also still says `ids.len()`. Fix: collect genuinely new ids into a `BTreeSet<EventId>` or increment `pinned` only when `set.insert(id)` returns true; update the failure table.

- MED [docs/design/lmdb/tests/insert.md:18](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a1e976d43e1097718/docs/design/lmdb/tests/insert.md:18): the split-out insert doc still documents the old impossible `WriteCounting<S: EventStore>` wrapper. Fix: replace this block with the `LmdbEventStore::with_write_observer` hook from `tests.md` §1.1 or link there.

- LOW [docs/design/lmdb/tests.md:80](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a1e976d43e1097718/docs/design/lmdb/tests.md:80): anchor typo `signationdelegation`; fix to `signaturedelegation`.

Verification: #1 scan ordering addressed; #4 D6 cite addressed; #5 `coverage()` signature addressed; #6 LOC split addressed at 184/123/57. #2 and #3 are only partially addressed due the findings above. `git diff --check` is clean.
